title: A Multi-Dimensional Evaluation of Hybrid Quantum Circuit Born Machines for Data-Scarce Clinical Imaging: Assessing Scalability, Generalization and Trainability
description: This MSc thesis investigates the application of Quantum Circuit Born Machines (QCBM) to generate synthetic clinical images when training data is limited. The research explores how quantum machine learning can address the critical challenge of data scarcity in medical imaging, particularly for rare diseases where obtaining large datasets is difficult. The study aims to demonstrate that QCBM models can produce high-quality, diverse medical images that maintain clinical relevance while preserving patient privacy, potentially enabling better AI training for diagnostic models in resource-constrained healthcare settings.
